title: Passing the finite-N Radon–Nikodym identity to the renormalization limit

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Step B→limit (global consistency).** The exact identity $\frac{dT_\psi^*\mu_N}{d\mu_N}=e^{X_N}$ holds at finite $N$ (finite-dim / trace-class approximation).

**Gap:** Justify that $\mu_N\to\mu$ and $T_\psi^*\mu_N\to T_\psi^*\mu$ in a topology compatible with convergence of Radon–Nikodym derivatives, i.e. weak convergence of measures + uniform integrability of $F_N$ $\Rightarrow$ $\frac{dT_\psi^*\mu}{d\mu}=\lim_N F_N$ in $L^1(\mu)$. Care is needed because $T_\psi^*$ (a smooth shift) does not commute trivially with the renormalization limit: the counterterms $c_N^{(1)}\sim N$, $c_N^{(2)}\sim\log N$ in $V_N(u)-V_N(u-\psi)$ generate $\psi$-dependent divergences that must cancel against the Wick subtractions before the limit.

**Why nontrivial:** without this step one only has equivalence of the *approximations*, not of $\mu$ and $T_\psi^*\mu$. Note related open issues q1-3/q1-4/q1-5 flag exactly such uncancelled-divergence / counterterm-normalization errors in a prior attempt.
